description: Reviewed subset fixture for comparing single-reflector vs Trinity quality

testCases.principleDescription: Map Before Territory: Always survey the existing structure before making changes
testCases.signalContext: Agent edited src/main.ts without reading it first, causing a merge conflict

singleReflectorOutput.badDecision: Edited src/main.ts without first reading its contents, leading to a merge conflict
singleReflectorOutput.betterDecision: Read src/main.ts to understand its current structure before making edits
singleReflectorOutput.rationale: Surveying the existing territory prevents conflicts and ensures edits integrate properly

trinityOutput.badDecision: Edited src/main.ts without first reading its contents, leading to a merge conflict with parallel changes
trinityOutput.betterDecision: Before editing, read src/main.ts to understand its current structure and identify any conflicting sections
trinityOutput.rationale: Surveying the existing territory before making changes prevents conflicts and ensures the edit integrates properly with the current implementation

testCases.notes: Trinity produces more specific badDecision and betterDecision with better rationale

testCases.principleDescription: Pain as Signal: Treat failures and errors as signals to pause and reflect
testCases.signalContext: Agent retries failing bash command 3 times without any diagnosis

singleReflectorOutput.rationale: Treating failures as signals prevents repeated failures

trinityOutput.badDecision: After bash command failed with 'command not found', immediately retried the exact same command without pausing to diagnose the root cause
trinityOutput.betterDecision: When the bash command fails, pause to check if the tool is installed, verify the path, or consult documentation before retrying
trinityOutput.rationale: Treating each failure as a signal to diagnose rather than blindly retry prevents repeated failures and respects the cost of each action attempt

testCases.notes: Single-reflector is generic; Trinity provides specific diagnosis steps

testCases.principleDescription: Evidence Over Intuition: Ground decisions in observable evidence
testCases.signalContext: Agent proceeded with a tool call despite receiving a gate block

trinityOutput.badDecision: Proceeded with a tool call despite receiving a gate block, bypassing the safety check
trinityOutput.betterDecision: Read the blocked operation documentation and obtain proper authorization before retrying the operation
trinityOutput.rationale: Respecting gate blocks prevents unintended system modifications and ensures alignment with operational constraints

testCases.notes: Single-reflector is too generic; Trinity is much more specific

summary.conclusion: Trinity consistently outperforms single-reflector on reviewed subset
